# Common Components

## Description

Each of the ROS reporting forms begins with an INFO section, which captures core information that is applicable to all fisheries regardless of gear type or data collection method. The general data fields provide the essential contextual framework for linking records across datasets, supporting quality control and validation processes.

## Metadata

The data fields in this section are organized into interconnected subsections that collectively summarize and identify an observed fishing trip. The start of the form is the metadata section where contact details are submitted. Data covers information about the submission and the person submitting the data, as well as information about the year of the data and the flag or CPC reporting. 

## Observer Information

This is followed by a section for observer information. The unique identifier for this section is the observed trip ID. Key elements of the subsequent fields include information about the duration of the trip and a summary of activities conducted, including fishing events conducted and observed, number of days fishing, searching, or lost, and where relevant, the reasons for lost days.   

```{r CommonComponents, fig.cap = "Common Components of ROS reporting forms. Each sheet contains a unique identifier (UI) to identify the trip. Subsetions, each containing individual fields are mapped. Mandatory subsections are shown in blue, while optional are shown in green.", fig.asp = 0.75, width = 8}
knitr::include_graphics("../../inputs/charts/IOTC_ROS_CommonComponents.png")
```
## Vessel Information

The third section of the form contains vessel information, with two subsections. Each section with vessel information contains an observed trip UI.   Required information focuses on vessel identification and personnel onboard. Subsections contain data on the vessel trip, which may differ from the observer trip, and vessel attributes. Vessel attribute data includes the electronics present on board, as well as fish storage and preservation methods available, and waste management capacity, all of which determine the capacity of the operations of the vessel. Sections after vessel attributes are specific to respective fishing gears.  

<hr style="border:1.5px solid black">
